definition:
φυσικός, -ή, -όν 
 (ψύσις), 
natural 
   (a) produced by nature, innate (Xen., Arist., al.); 
   (b) according to nature (Arist., Diod., al.): opposite to παρὰ φύσιν, Rom.1:26-27; 
   (with) governed by mere natural instinct (of. Plut., Mor., 706 A): ζῷα γεγεννημένα φ. (RV, born mere animals; Mayor, born creatures of instinct; ICC, animals born of mere nature), 2Pe.2:12,†
 (AS)
